name: Spec sheet
when_to_use: 需要清晰交付某一个产品、设备、方案或文件的成组规格时用它：适合技术参数、采购附件、交付物摘要和详情页侧栏。若重点是多个候选项横向胜负，应改用 comparison-table；若指标本身需要显示相对强弱，应改用 scorecard-bars。
why_valuable: 普通 label:value 列表很容易丢失单位、把长值挤乱、让版本号等机器值与说明文字混在一起；本模板固定单对象、分组带状标题、单位层级、等宽值和 ✓/✗/partial 状态语义，窄屏仍保持可扫读。

data_in.note: data 描述一个对象的规格：groups 按显示顺序提供分组，每组 fields 是 label:value 行。value 接受 string 或 number 并默认等宽显示；unit 与 value 分开以保持数值对齐；mono:false 用于自然语言值；status 可附加只读达标语义。空 groups 显示空态；不对值做换算或推断。

schema.title?: string —— 对象名称；省略时显示 “Specification”
schema.subtitle?: string —— 名称下方的一行型号、用途或版本说明
schema.badge?: string —— 右上角短状态，例如 “Release candidate”
schema.groups: {label:string,fields:{label:string,value:string|number,unit?:string,note?:string,status?:'yes'|'no'|'partial',mono?:boolean}[]}[] —— 有序规格分组；label 是分组带标题
schema.groups[].fields[].label: string —— 属性名
schema.groups[].fields[].value: string|number —— 主值；默认以等宽字体显示
schema.groups[].fields[].unit?: string —— 与主值分离显示的单位，不应重复写进 value
schema.groups[].fields[].note?: string —— 值下方的短限定说明
schema.groups[].fields[].status?: 'yes'|'no'|'partial' —— 可选只读状态，分别显示绿色 ✓、红色 ✗、琥珀色部分满足
schema.groups[].fields[].mono?: boolean —— false 改用系统字体；默认 true

spec.note: 按分组与字段数量自然增高；两列 label:value 在 520px 以下折为单列。无 vh/百分比高度，也不读取载体尺寸。

example._why: 示例覆盖多分组、单位、自然语言值、备注和三态中的 ✓/partial，能直接看出它是单个硬件交付物而非比较表。

uplink: display-only：规格、状态与单位均为单向展示，不定义任何向 AI 回传的事件。

carriers.artifact: supported:boot.artifact 已在 sandboxed iframe 中以 example.minimal 原样渲染验证；CSS 与 DOM 全部位于自有 shadow root
carriers.widget: supported:与 artifact 同一模板，仅 parent 改为 shadowRoot；可在载体 shadow 内再挂自有 shadow
carriers.codex: supported:与已验证的 artifact boot 相同，document.getElementById('viz') 为唯一载体挂载点

isolation.evidence: self-authored inline-template, owns its shadow, zero global footprint; rendered-verified

en.when_to_use: Use it when you need to clearly deliver grouped specifications for one product, device, proposal, or file: suitable for technical parameters, procurement attachments, deliverable summaries, and detail-page sidebars. If the focus is head-to-head results across multiple candidates, use comparison-table instead; if the metrics themselves need relative-strength encoding, use scorecard-bars.
en.why_valuable: Ordinary label:value lists easily lose units, crush long values, and mix machine values such as version numbers with prose. This template fixes a single-object model, banded group headings, unit hierarchy, monospaced values, and yes/no/partial status semantics while remaining scannable on narrow screens.

data_in.note: data describes one object's specifications: groups are supplied in display order, and each group's fields are label:value rows. value accepts string or number and is monospaced by default; unit is separated from value to align numbers; mono:false is for natural-language values; status adds read-only compliance semantics. Empty groups displays an empty state. Values are not converted or inferred.

schema.title?: string -- Object name; displays "Specification" when omitted
schema.subtitle?: string -- One-line model, purpose, or version description below the name
schema.badge?: string -- Short top-right status, such as "Release candidate"
schema.groups: {label:string,fields:{label:string,value:string|number,unit?:string,note?:string,status?:'yes'|'no'|'partial',mono?:boolean}[]}[] -- Ordered specification groups; label is the group-band heading
schema.groups[].fields[].label: string -- Attribute name
schema.groups[].fields[].value: string|number -- Main value; monospaced by default
schema.groups[].fields[].unit?: string -- Unit displayed separately from the main value; do not repeat it in value
schema.groups[].fields[].note?: string -- Short qualifier below the value
schema.groups[].fields[].status?: 'yes'|'no'|'partial' -- Optional read-only status, displayed as green yes, red no, or amber partial
schema.groups[].fields[].mono?: boolean -- false uses the system font; default true

spec.note: Grows naturally with the number of groups and fields; two-column label:value rows collapse to one column below 520px. No vh or percentage height, and no carrier-size reads.
